elements.sent-2411-001: In this work, we obtain the description of all homogeneous anti-Rota-Baxter operators on Witt and Virasoro algebras.
elements.sent-2411-002: Moreover, we describe anti-Rota-Baxter operators on three-dimensional simple Lie algebra $sl_2.$ Keywords: Anti-Rota-Baxter operators, Witt algebra, Virasoro algebras.. MSC (2020):17A36,17B38,17B68.
elements.sent-2411-003: The concept of Rota-Baxter operators is used in combinatorics and probability theory.
elements.sent-2411-004: Later, it was used in algebra.
elements.sent-2411-005: In 1960, Glen Baxter introduced a Baxter equation, which satisfies a certain property related to probability distributions \cite{Baxter , using Rota-Baxter operators, \begin{equation}2(a(aT))T=(a^2b)T+(aT)^2.\end{equation}. In 1983, M.A.}
elements.sent-2411-006: Semenov-Tian-Shansky studied the case of Rota-Baxter equations with weight 0 from the perspective of Lie algebras, interpreting them as Yang-Baxter equations \cite{S .}
elements.sent-2411-007: The concept of Rota-Baxter operators goes back to Richard Baxters and Gian-Carlo Rota and found several fields of mathematics \cite{Rota-Kung .}
elements.sent-2411-008: Rota–Baxter operator of weight $0$ on a Lie algebra is exactly the operator form of the classical Yang–Baxter equation (CYBE), which was regarded as a ``classical limit'' of the quantum Yang–Baxter equation \cite{Belavin , whereas the latter is also an important topic in many fields such as symplectic geometry, integrable systems, quantum groups and quantum field theory \cite{GS, Guo1, Guo2 .}}
elements.sent-2411-009: By the 21st century, Rota-Baxter operators experienced a period of advancement.
elements.sent-2411-010: This can be seen in the algebraic renormalization method in quantum field theory, dendriform algebras, the associative analog of the classical Yang-Baxter equation, and it works on systems with mixed commutative and non-commutative products.
elements.sent-2411-011: A linear operator $R: A \rightarrow A$ is called a Rota-Baxter operator on $A$ of weight $\lambda$ if the following identity \begin{equation}[R(x),R(y)] = R([R(x),y]+[x,R(y)]+ \lambda xy),\end{equation} holds for any $x, y\in A.$ It is known that if $R$ is a Rota-Baxter operator weight $\lambda$ with $\lambda \neq 0,$ then the operator $\lambda^{-1 R$ is a Rota-Baxter operator of weight 1.}
elements.sent-2411-012: Moreover, if $d$ is an invertible derivation of an algebra $A$, then $d^{-1}$ forms a Rota-Baxter operator of weight zero \cite{Guo2 .}
elements.sent-2411-013: Initially, Filippov and Hopkins introduced the concept of anti-derivations in their works \cite{fil95, hop .}
elements.sent-2411-014: Later, Filippov generalized the notions of derivations and anti-derivations introducing the notion of $\delta$-derivation \cite{fil98 .}
elements.sent-2411-015: Later, the concept of $\delta$-derivations has been further studied, giving rise to significant results \cite{fil99, zak22, pasha10 .}
elements.sent-2411-016: In particular, it was proved that prime Lie algebras, do not have nonzero $\delta$-derivations (provided $\delta \neq 1, -1, 0, \frac 1 2$), and alternative, non-Lie Malcev algebras with certain restrictions of $\mathbb{F}$ have no non-trivial $\delta$-derivation.
elements.sent-2411-017: Zusmanovich in \cite{pasha10 proved that a prime Lie superalgebra has no non-trivial $\delta$-(super)derivations for $\delta\neq 1, -1, 0, \frac 1 2$.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Based on the principal relation between derivations and Rota-Baxter operators it can be introduced the notion of $\delta$-Rota-Baxter operator weight $0$ with the condition $[R(x),R(y)] = \delta R([R(x),y]+[x,R(y)]).$ Similarly, to the derivation case, any invertible $\delta$-derivation of the algebra $A$ gives $\delta$-Rota-Baxter operator weight $0$.
elements.sent-2411-019: On the other hand, the structure for constructing anti-$\mathcal{O}$-operators was developed by Kupershmidt \cite{Ku , and according to it, the following definition of the anti-Rota-Baxter operator arises.}
elements.sent-2411-020: An anti-Rota-Baxter operator $R$ is called a {\bf strong if R satisfies \begin{equation}\label{Rb-strong} [[R(x), R(y)], z] + [[R(y), R(z)], x] + [[R(z), R(x)], y] = 0, \quad \forall x, y, z \in \mathfrak{g}.\end{equation}.}
elements.formula-2411-003: \begin{equation}\label{Rb-strong} [[R(x), R(y)], z] + [[R(y), R(z)], x] + [[R(z), R(x)], y] = 0, \quad \forall x, y, z \in g.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-021: In this paper, we obtain anti-Rota-Baxter operators on the Witt and Virasoro algebras by using this method.
elements.sent-2411-022: Moreover, we classify anti-Rota-Baxter operators on three-dimensional simple Lie algebra $sl_2.$ It should be noted that Rota-Baxter operators on $sl_2$ were obtained by J.. Pei, C.
elements.section-2411-002: Homogeneous Anti-Rota-Baxter operators on the Witt and Virasoro algebra
elements.sent-2411-025: We also assume that $\mathbb F=\mathbb C$, is the complex field since both the Witt and Virasoro algebras are defined over $\mathbb C$.
elements.sent-2411-026: A {\it homogeneous operator $R$ with degree $k$} on the Witt algebra $W$ is a linear operator on $W$ satisfying \begin{equation}R(W_m)\subset W_{m+k}, \quad \forall m\inZ.\end{equation}.
